prompt: A Meridian Enterprise Operations & Document Management system that includes controlled document libraries, configurable operations governance, and auditable sales/returns workflows for regulated back-office teams running fully offline. The backend uses Laravel to expose resource-based APIs and MySQL to persist entities and audit trails. Core API capability domains include Authentication and Roles (local username + password only; password minimum 12 characters, at least 1 upper, 1 lower, 1 number, lockout for 15 minutes after 5 failed attempts), Document & Materials APIs (create/query/update versioned policy documents and common forms; enforce access control by role and department; support online preview metadata and controlled downloads; one-click archiving that freezes a document to read-only and stamps an archive date), Attachment & Evidence APIs (multi-file attachment to business records; accept only PDF/DOCX/XLSX/PNG/JPG, max 25 MB per file, max 20 files per record; validate file headers/MIME consistency; compute SHA-256 fingerprints for deduplication; encrypt-at-rest; manage validity periods so evidence auto-expires after a configured number of days, default 365; generate expiring external links for offline sharing within a LAN with a max TTL of 72 hours and single-use option), Operations & Configuration Center APIs (configure homepage modules/ad slots, campaign/landing topics, coupon/promotion rules, blacklist/whitelist, and purchase limits; all configuration changes are versioned with canary rollout to a selectable subset of stores/users capped at 10% for 24 hours before full promotion), Workflow & Approval APIs (template-driven multi-level approvals with conditional branching by amount thresholds and event type; support parallel sign-off, add-approver, reassignment, withdrawal before final approval, and rejection with mandatory reason; node SLAs default to 2 business days with reminder events placed into local to-do queues), Sales Issue and Return/Exchange APIs (sales/issue document lifecycle state machine: draft, reviewed, completed, voided; document numbers unique, date-prefixed, and sequential per site; outbound linkage allowed only after final approval; returns/exchanges require reason codes and trigger inventory rollback rules with configurable restock fee, default 10% for non-defective returns within 30 days). Data is modeled with MySQL tables for users, roles, departments, documents, document_versions, attachments, attachment_links, configuration_sets, configuration_versions, workflow_templates, workflow_instances, workflow_nodes, approvals, to_do_items, sales_documents, sales_line_items, returns, and inventory_movements; all primary identifiers are UUIDs, with unique indexes on username, document_number, and (document_id, version_number). Enums constrain states (e.g., workflow_status, sales_status) and policy types (coupon vs promotion vs limit). Every write operation is auditable via immutable append-only audit_events capturing actor_id, timestamp, action, before/after hashes, and correlation_id for idempotency; repeated requests with the same correlation_id must not create duplicates. Security requires role-based authorization, field-level masking for sensitive notes, AES-256 encryption for attachment payloads, and watermarking rules recorded at download time (watermark text includes username and timestamp, applied to PDFs only). The system must run on a single Docker-hosted machine with no external dependencies, performing all validation locally (no cloud OCR or online antivirus); file integrity checks rely on format validation and fingerprinting. Non-functional targets include p95 API latency under 300 ms for typical queries on 1 million records, support 200 concurrent users on a single host, daily local backups with 14-day retention, and structured logs plus metrics (request timing, queue depth, failed approvals) retained for 90 days for offline troubleshooting.
